title: Embeddedness governs: decision-making networks in five Shanghai communities
abstract: This study proposes a full network approach to examine power structure and state penetrating strategies in community governance in urban China. We collect network data from diverse stakeholders regarding routine and crisis decision-making scenarios in five Shanghai communities. Results reveal that both structural and relational embeddedness matter. Structurally, the decision-making networks feature a polycentric structure with a core-periphery tendency. Relationally, a strong tie is the most consistent predictor of a stakeholder's central network position. This study demonstrates the intricate power structures at the grassroots level. It also illuminates the state's leverage on embeddedness to gain decision-making power in community governance.
authors_text: Tian, Felicia F.; Zhou, Wenyu; Chen, Lin
